other_observers: RMBO trainees and trainers including Tony Leukering, Rich Levad, Coen Dexter, Mark Peterson, Glenn Walbek, et al.;
duration: Several sightings of a few minutes each
specific_location: Lathrop State Park, Yucca Campground, Group Loop B. South of southern loop road in gully with flowing water. Also seen up the gully to the west.
description: A blocky, large sparrow. Bill mostly dull yellowish orange, mandible perhaps a shade paler, culmen dark gray. Black cap above mid eye level, eye is half in black cap and half in the mostly unmarked gray of the rest of the face and throat. Lower half of eye has thin white border. Eye is dark. The front half of the wide median crown stripe is golden yellow. The rear half abruptly turns pale grayish white. The belly is mostly unmarked gray, transitioning to browner tones on the flanks. Feet and legs are orangeish pink. Wings are mostly dark edged brown, with thin, broken wing bars formed in the tips of the median and greater coverts. Tail is mostly featureless dark grayish brown.Behaviors: The bird was feeding amongst catkins on a budding cottonwood tree when first found. Later it was seen foraging on the ground amongst rabbitbrush plants and at the base of a juniper. Inbetween sightings it appeared to retreat into heavy brushy willows and other dense vegetation in a small gully with flowing water.Call: I heard a few call notes but could not separate them from White crowned Sparrow with certainty.Plumage: adult alternate
photographer: Photographer: Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker
